title: Direct Disbursement
text: Direct Disbursement: Loans must flow from the lender's bank account directly to the borrower's account without any third-party pool accounts.

title: LSP Transparency
text: LSP Transparency: Regulated Entities (REs) must maintain an updated public website with a dedicated section listing all their Digital Lending products, DLAs, engaged LSPs and their respective roles. DLAs must clearly display the RE's name, loan terms, and applicable charges when presenting loan offers to borrowers, and must be linked back to the RE's website. Additionally, REs must ensure impartiality by displaying all matched loan offers without promoting any specific lender's product.

title: Purpose-Limited & Auditable Consent
text: Purpose-Limited & Auditable Consent: Regulated Entities (REs) must obtain borrower consent for data collection only for specific, necessary purposes — primarily credit assessment — and must maintain auditable records of such consent. Data collection must be minimal, transparent, and not reused beyond the original purpose without fresh consent.

title: Cooling-Off Period
text: Cooling-Off Period: Borrowers are entitled to a minimum 1-day cooling-off period after loan origination during which they may exit the loan without penalty — subject to payment of the principal amount and pro-rata interest. This period applies regardless of loan tenor and is designed to provide borrowers with a window to reconsider their commitment.

title: Audit Trail
text: Audit Trail: All interactions between the Lending Service Provider (LSP) and the borrower must be recorded and accessible for audit.

title: Multi-Lender Platforms (NEW)
text: Multi-Lender Platforms (NEW): LSPs partnering with multiple lenders must disclose all potential lenders transparently and present loan offers without bias.  [UPDATED]

category: Category 2: The New Key Fact Statement (KFS)
title: Standardized KFS
text: Standardized KFS: Every retail and MSME loan must include a one-page KFS summary before the loan agreement is signed.

category: Category 2: The New Key Fact Statement (KFS)
title: APR Disclosure
text: APR Disclosure: The KFS must show the Annual Percentage Rate (APR), which includes interest plus all mandatory fees like processing and insurance.

category: Category 2: The New Key Fact Statement (KFS)
title: Zero Hidden Charges
text: Zero Hidden Charges: Any fee (legal fees, valuation fees) not mentioned in the KFS cannot be charged to the borrower later.

category: Category 2: The New Key Fact Statement (KFS)
title: Language Access
text: Language Access: The KFS and loan terms must be provided in a language the borrower understands (English / Hindi / Vernacular).

category: Category 2: The New Key Fact Statement (KFS)
title: Acknowledge Receipt
text: Acknowledge Receipt: Lenders must obtain the borrower’s explicit acknowledgment — either digital or physical — confirming they have understood the KFS contents before finalizing the loan agreement.

title: Penal Charges, Not Interest
text: Penal Charges, Not Interest: Lenders cannot charge 'Penal Interest' (which compounds). They can only charge a fixed 'Penal Charge' for defaults.

title: No Penalty on Penalty
text: No Penalty on Penalty: Penal charges cannot be added to the principal; therefore, no interest can be charged on the penalty itself.

title: Floating Rate Switch
text: Floating Rate Switch: During an interest rate hike, lenders must offer borrowers an option to switch to a Fixed Interest Rate.

title: EMI vs. Tenor Choice
text: EMI vs. Tenor Choice: Borrowers must be given the choice to either increase their EMI amount or elongate the loan duration when rates rise.

title: External Benchmark Linking (EBLR)
text: External Benchmark Linking (EBLR): Floating rate loans for individuals must be linked to an external benchmark (like the RBI Repo Rate) for transparency.

title: 30-Day Document Release
text: 30-Day Document Release: Lenders must release all original movable/immovable property documents within 30 days of full loan repayment.

title: Delayed Release Penalty
text: Delayed Release Penalty: If the bank fails to return original documents within 30 days, they must pay the borrower ₹5,000 per day of delay.

title: Loss of Documents
text: Loss of Documents: In case of loss of original title deeds, the lender must pay for the duplicate copies AND pay compensation of ₹5,000/day after the 30-day limit.

title: Choice of Branch
text: Choice of Branch: Borrowers must be given the option to collect their original movable and immovable property documents from either the branch where the loan was serviced or any other branch or office of the lender where the documents are available, as per the borrower’s preference.

title: Fortnightly Reporting
text: Fortnightly Reporting: Banks must update borrower data to Credit Bureaus (CIBIL, etc.) every 15 days (previously 30 days).

title: Free Credit Report
text: Free Credit Report: Individuals are entitled to one Free Full Credit Report (FFCR) per year from every Credit Information Company.

title: 30-Day Correction Window
text: 30-Day Correction Window: Errors in credit reports must be rectified within 30 days of a complaint, or the bank/bureau pays compensation.

title: Inquiry Alerts
text: Inquiry Alerts: Lenders must send an SMS or Email alert to the borrower every time their credit report is accessed for a loan application.

title: Calling Hours
text: Calling Hours: Recovery agents can only contact borrowers between 08:00 AM and 07:00 PM.

title: No Harassment
text: No Harassment: NBFCs must avoid all forms of harassment — including use of muscle power, persistent contact at odd hours, or intimidation — during loan recovery. This applies across all channels, including digital messaging, to ensure fair and respectful treatment of borrowers.

title: Agent ID
text: Agent ID: Any agent visiting a borrower must carry a valid ID card and an authorization letter from the Bank/NBFC.

title: Privacy Protection
text: Privacy Protection: Recovery agents must not harass or intrude on the privacy of borrowers’ family, friends, or referees — including through inappropriate messages, threatening calls, or public disclosure of debt details.

title: Gold Loan LTV
text: Gold Loan LTV: The maximum Loan-to-Value (LTV) ratio for consumption loans against gold collateral is tiered:

title: Guideline
text: 85% for loans ≤ ₹2.5 lakh

title: Guideline
text: 80% for loans > ₹2.5 lakh and ≤ ₹5 lakh

title: Guideline
text: 75% for loans > ₹5 lakh

category_note: (Note: LTV must be maintained throughout the loan tenor.)
title: Home Loan Foreclosure
text: Home Loan Foreclosure: No foreclosure or prepayment penalties are allowed on floating-rate home loans for individuals.

category_note: (Note: LTV must be maintained throughout the loan tenor.)
title: Credit Card Billing Grace
text: Credit Card Billing Grace: Borrowers get a 3-day grace period after the due date before they are reported as 'late' to credit bureaus.

category_note: (Note: LTV must be maintained throughout the loan tenor.)
title: Credit Card Closure
text: Credit Card Closure: If a bank fails to close a credit card within 7 working days of a request, they pay a penalty of ₹500/day.

category_note: (Note: LTV must be maintained throughout the loan tenor.)
title: Unsolicited Card Upgrades
text: Unsolicited Card Upgrades: Activating a credit card or upgrading a limit without explicit customer consent leads to a heavy RBI penalty on the bank.

category_note: (Note: LTV must be maintained throughout the loan tenor.)
title: Education Loan Moratorium
text: Education Loan Moratorium: Repayment includes a mandatory moratorium of course duration + 1 year (12 months) — not 6 to 12 months. Banks may offer additional short-term moratoriums (up to 6 months at a time) for underemployment/unemployment, subject to discretion.

title: MFI Household Income
text: MFI Household Income: Microfinance loans are restricted to households with an annual income up to ₹3 Lakh.

title: MFI Prepayment
text: MFI Prepayment: No prepayment penalty is allowed on any microfinance loan, regardless of the interest rate type.

title: V-CIP (Video KYC)
text: V-CIP (Video KYC): Video-based KYC is legally equivalent to physical face-to-face verification for loan onboarding.

title: Small Account Limits
text: Small Account Limits: KYC-lite (Small) accounts have a balance limit of ₹50,000 and total credits of ₹1 Lakh per year.

title: Dormant Account Charging
text: Dormant Account Charging: Banks are not permitted to levy any penal charges — including for non-maintenance of minimum balance — on accounts classified as inoperative. Dormancy alone does not justify fees; only customer-initiated transactions reactivate the account.

title: Integrated Ombudsman
text: Integrated Ombudsman: If a complaint is not resolved by the bank within 30 days, the borrower can escalate to the RBI Ombudsman via the CMS portal (https://cms.rbi.org.in) or by physical complaint.  [UPDATED]

title: Internal Ombudsman
text: Internal Ombudsman: Large NBFCs and Banks must appoint an Internal Ombudsman to review all rejected customer complaints.

title: Nodal Officer
text: Nodal Officer: Only Lending Service Providers (LSPs) with direct borrower interface must appoint a dedicated Nodal Grievance Redressal Officer — their contact details must be accessible via the app. The regulated entity (RE) remains ultimately accountable for all complaints, regardless of LSP involvement.

title: Right to Reason
text: Right to Reason: A borrower has the right to know the specific reason for a loan application rejection in writing.

title: Wilful Defaulter Notice
text: Wilful Defaulter Notice: The bank must issue a 21-day show-cause notice to the borrower. After the Identification Committee reviews the response, the Review Committee provides 15 days for written representation and offers a personal hearing before making a final decision.
